loop_cards.who_has: the worst-case time complexity of binary search?
the data structure that is LIFO?
the traversal that outputs a BST in ascending order?
the average-case complexity of quick sort?
the search that requires the list to be sorted?
the worst-case complexity of quick sort?
the data structure that is FIFO?
the path-finding algorithm that uses a heuristic?
the complexity of pushing onto a stack?
the sort that picks a pivot and partitions the list?
the best-case complexity of insertion sort?
the traversal order Left to Right to Node?
the sort that repeatedly swaps adjacent elements?
the algorithm that finds the shortest path to all nodes with no heuristic?
the sort that recursively splits then merges sublists?

quickfire.clue: The time complexity of binary search (average/worst).
The time complexity of a single loop over n items.
The worst-case time complexity of quick sort.
The guaranteed time complexity of merge sort in all cases.
The time complexity of pushing onto a stack.
The search algorithm that needs the data sorted first.
The sort that picks a pivot and partitions the list.
The traversal: Left to Node to Right.
The path-finding algorithm that adds a heuristic h(n) to g(n).
The data structure that is FIFO.
The sort that bubbles the largest value to the end each pass.
The space complexity of merge sort.
